openmeter/billing/worker/advance.purpose: Batch auto-advance loop for StandardInvoices: polls three disjoint invoice states (DraftWaitingAutoApproval, DraftWaitingForCollection, stuck-advanceable), merges/deduplicates, then fans out parallel goroutines in lo.Chunk batches to call billing.Service.AdvanceInvoice. The scheduled polling counterpart to the event-driven asyncadvance handler.

patterns.name: Config struct constructor with nil validation
patterns.description: NewAdvancer takes a Config and errors if BillingService or Logger is nil.
patterns.example: func NewAdvancer(config Config) (*AutoAdvancer, error) { if config.BillingService == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("billing service is required") } }

patterns.name: Batched parallel fan-out with errChan + WaitGroup
patterns.description: lo.Chunk splits work into batches; each batch spawns goroutines writing to a buffered errChan sized to total invoice count; sync.OnceFunc closes the channel; errors.Join collects failures.
patterns.example: errChan := make(chan error, len(invoices)); closeErrChan := sync.OnceFunc(func() { close(errChan) }); defer closeErrChan()

patterns.name: ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance is a non-fatal sentinel
patterns.description: When AdvanceInvoice returns billing.ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance, fetch details, log a warning, and return nil — never propagate.
patterns.example: if errors.Is(err, billing.ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance) { a.logger.WarnContext(ctx, "invoice cannot be advanced", logArgs...); return invoice, nil }

patterns.name: List consolidation with lo.UniqBy before batching
patterns.description: ListInvoicesToAdvance merges three list calls and deduplicates by invoice ID before batching to prevent double-advances.
patterns.example: return lo.UniqBy(allInvoices, func(i billing.StandardInvoice) string { return i.ID }), nil

patterns.name: Context with cancel wraps batch runs
patterns.description: All() creates a cancellable child context so in-flight goroutines cancel when the parent cancels.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package — AutoAdvancer with All(), ListInvoicesToAdvance(), ListInvoicesPendingAutoAdvance/PendingCollection/StuckInvoicesNeedingAdvance(), AdvanceInvoice(), NewAdvancer().
key_file_guides.watch_for: batchSize=0 means single batch; errChan capacity must equal len(invoices) not len(batches) or sends block; ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance must stay a warning return (nil).

anti_patterns: Calling billing.Adapter directly — all DB access goes through billing.Service.
Propagating billing.ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance as an error from AdvanceInvoice.
Forgetting lo.UniqBy deduplication on the merged list, causing double-advances.
Using context.Background() inside goroutines instead of the caller-supplied ctx.
Sizing errChan to len(batches) instead of len(invoices) — goroutine sends deadlock.

decisions.decision: Three separate list queries merged with lo.UniqBy instead of a single compound query.
decisions.rationale: The three states have distinct filter shapes in ListStandardInvoicesInput; merging at the query level would need OR semantics the adapter does not support cleanly.

decisions.decision: Batched parallel goroutines rather than sequential advance.
decisions.rationale: Advancement is per-invoice and independent; parallelism cuts wall-clock time for large namespaces while batchSize controls memory pressure.

code_examples.scenario: Advance all eligible invoices in batches of 50

openmeter/billing/worker/asyncadvance.purpose: Watermill event handler for async invoice advancement: receives billing.AdvanceStandardInvoiceEvent from the system Kafka topic and calls billing.Service.AdvanceInvoice once, treating ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance as a benign late-message condition. Requires the billing service configured with ForegroundAdvancementStrategy to avoid infinite retry loops.

patterns.name: Config.Validate() guards strategy mismatch at construction
patterns.description: New() calls Validate() which rejects any BillingService not using ForegroundAdvancementStrategy — prevents an infinite loop where background advancement re-emits the same event.
patterns.example: if c.BillingService.GetAdvancementStrategy() != billing.ForegroundAdvancementStrategy { return errors.New("billing service must have foreground advancement strategy or we are creating an infinite loop") }

patterns.name: Single-event Handle() matching the NoPublisherHandler closure
patterns.description: Handler.Handle(ctx, *billing.AdvanceStandardInvoiceEvent) is the only exported method beyond New(), matching the worker router's expected closure signature.

patterns.name: ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance -> warn + nil
patterns.description: Late Kafka messages for already-advanced invoices log a warning and return nil (no requeue); all other errors propagate.
patterns.example: if errors.Is(err, billing.ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance) { h.logger.WarnContext(ctx, "invoice cannot advance (most probably a late message)", "invoice_id", event.Invoice); return nil }

key_file_guides.role: Entire package — Config, Config.Validate(), New(), Handler.Handle().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Forgetting the strategy validation creates an infinite loop: background strategy re-emits advance events this handler re-processes forever; Handler is intentionally stateless beyond service + logger.

anti_patterns: Wiring this handler with a BackgroundAdvancementStrategy BillingService — creates an infinite Kafka consume-emit loop.
Returning ErrInvoiceCannotAdvance from Handle() — causes Watermill to nack and requeue indefinitely.
Adding mutable state to Handler — the struct is intentionally stateless.
Using context.Background() inside Handle() instead of the event's ctx parameter.

decisions.decision: Strategy guard in Config.Validate() rather than a runtime check in Handle().
decisions.rationale: Misconfiguration creates a hard-to-detect runtime infinite loop; failing fast at construction surfaces the bug on startup.

code_examples.scenario: Wire asyncadvance handler into a Watermill grouphandler

openmeter/billing/worker/collect.purpose: Batch invoice collection loop: lists GatheringInvoices whose NextCollectionAt is due (including nil-NextCollectionAt legacy invoices), deduplicates by customer, filters locked namespaces, then fans out parallel CollectCustomerInvoice calls triggering billing.Service.InvoicePendingLines with partial lines disabled.

patterns.name: Config struct constructor with nil checks
patterns.description: NewInvoiceCollector validates GatheringInvoiceService, BillingService, and Logger non-nil; LockedNamespaces is optional.
patterns.example: func NewInvoiceCollector(config Config) (*InvoiceCollector, error) { if config.GatheringInvoiceService == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf(...) } }

patterns.name: Input types carry Validate() checked at entry
patterns.description: ListCollectableInvoicesInput and CollectCustomerInvoiceInput each have Validate() checked at the top of their callers before any service calls.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid input: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Sentinel errors drive recovery not failure
patterns.description: ErrNamespaceLocked -> warn + nil; ErrInvoiceCreateNoLines -> warn + RecalculateGatheringInvoices + nil. Only unknown errors propagate.
patterns.example: if errors.Is(err, billing.ErrNamespaceLocked) { a.logger.WarnContext(ctx, "namespace is locked, skipping", ...); return nil, nil }

patterns.name: InvoicePendingLines always with PartialInvoiceLinesDisabled
patterns.description: System-initiated collection always uses billing.WithPartialInvoiceLinesDisabled() to prevent progressive billing during automated collection.

patterns.name: Locked namespace exclusion before fan-out
patterns.description: All() filters customer IDs whose namespace is in a.lockedNamespaces via slices.Contains before building batches.
patterns.example: customerIDs = lo.Filter(lo.Uniq(customerIDs), func(id customer.CustomerID, _ int) bool { return !slices.Contains(a.lockedNamespaces, id.Namespace) })

patterns.name: Batched parallel fan-out (same as advance)
patterns.description: lo.Chunk by batchSize; goroutines per item write to a buffered errChan sized to total customer count; sync.OnceFunc closes; errors.Join collects.
patterns.example: errChan := make(chan error, len(customerIDs)); closeErrChan := sync.OnceFunc(func() { close(errChan) }); defer closeErrChan()

key_file_guides.role: Entire package — InvoiceCollector with All(), ListCollectableInvoices(), CollectCustomerInvoice(), NewInvoiceCollector(); input types with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: ListCollectableInvoices uses a filter.FilterTime OR clause to include nil NextCollectionAt — removing the nil branch silently skips legacy invoices; errChan must be sized to len(customerIDs); lo.Uniq must run on customerIDs before batching.

anti_patterns: Calling InvoicePendingLines without billing.WithPartialInvoiceLinesDisabled() — allows forbidden progressive billing in system collection.
Treating ErrNamespaceLocked or ErrInvoiceCreateNoLines as hard errors — both have recovery paths.
Skipping the locked-namespace filter before fan-out — redundant errors for every customer in a locked namespace.
Using context.Background() inside goroutines instead of the parent ctx.
Forgetting lo.Uniq on customerIDs before batching — a customer with multiple gathering invoices gets collected twice.

decisions.decision: Collect at customer granularity, not invoice granularity.
decisions.rationale: InvoicePendingLines operates per-customer and may merge multiple pending lines into one invoice; iterating by invoice ID would cause double-collect races.

decisions.decision: ErrInvoiceCreateNoLines triggers RecalculateGatheringInvoices instead of failing.
decisions.rationale: This state indicates stale gathering-invoice state; a recalculation heals it without surfacing an error or alert noise.

code_examples.scenario: Collect all due gathering invoices in batches of 100
